creator: e94pasch@yahoo.com
text:
Hello,

recently some cable providers in the US offer plans beyond the traditional 1Gbit/s limit of normal local LAN networks.
In my setup the cable modem Arris S33 comes with a 2.5Gbit/s port which is connected to my FreeBSD (Opnsense 12.1)based router via 10Gbit/s port.
After my upgrade i adjusted the traffic shaper (FQ_CODL enabled) based on my previous settings to go beyond 1Gbit/s but tests showed that there may be a limit of 1GBit/s regardless of settings and adjustments on my side. When i turn off the shaper i'll get 1.4GBit/s throughput (download).
Thank you and br.
